title: Parenting Skills Classes * Offender/Ex-Offender Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Child Care & Parenting > Parenting Skills Classes * Offender/Ex-Offender Issues 2 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues African American Community At Risk Families Child Abuse Issues Disabilities Issues Divorce/Custody/Support Issues Domestic Violence Issues Fathers Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorders Hispanic/Latino Community Mothers Native American Community Offender/Ex-Offender Issues Sexual Assault Issues Substance Use Disorder Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details PATHFINDER NETWORK PATHFINDER NETWORK CENTER FOR FAMILY SUCCESS PARENTING INSIDE OUT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Parenting curriculum focused on the needs of families who have been involved in the criminal justice and/or child welfare systems. Through interactive coaching, role play, and discussion, parents expand their understanding of child development, learn how to solve problems, and develop the skill of emotion regulation. Parents may receive training while incarcerated or in the community. Additional coaching and supported parenting time is available to some that have complement classroom instruction. Participating families can work with parent advocates to create family plans and connect with community resources and services. 7305 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Parents involved in the criminal justice and/or child welfare systems Hours: Classes are generally held on weeknights from 6pm-8pm and meet twice per week for 12 weeks Email (503) 892-5396 1008 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PATHFINDER NETWORK PARENTING INSIDE OUT
